title: cissus subaphylla
intro: Cissus subaphylla, also known as the vine of the gods or the wood apple, is a type of climber plant native to the tropical forests of Asia and Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it effective in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Cissus subaphylla include its ability to improve bone density, reduce joint pain and inflammation, and support digestive health. In herbal preparations, Cissus subaphylla is often used as an extract, powder, or tea, and can be found in various supplements and traditional medicine formulations.
